title: Haemul Pajeon Seafood Pancake

seo.title: Authentic Haemul Pajeon Recipe (Korean Seafood Pancake)
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make the best authentic Haemul Pajeon recipe at home! This crispy Korean seafood pancake is packed with fresh seafood and vegetables. Get the easy step-by-step guide.
seo.h1: Authentic Haemul Pajeon Recipe: Korean Seafood Pancake
h2Headings: Ingredients You'll Need for Haemul Pajeon
Step-by-Step Guide to Making Haemul Pajeon
Tips for a Perfectly Crispy Haemul Pajeon
seo.imageAlt: Crispy Haemul Pajeon, an authentic Korean seafood pancake, served with dipping sauce.
seo.imageCaption: Master the art of making Haemul Pajeon, the beloved Korean seafood pancake, with our easy recipe!

description: Haemul Pajeon is a beloved Korean savory pancake, crispy on the outside and tender within, loaded with an abundance of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. This classic dish is a delightful culinary experience, perfect as an appetizer or a light meal, often enjoyed with a tangy dipping sauce. Its irresistible texture and rich flavors make it a favorite for gatherings and a staple in Korean cuisine.

ingredients.notes: cleaned and chopped into bite-sized pieces

instructions.title: Prepare the Batter
instructions.text: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, salt, and white pepper. Gradually add the cold water or broth and egg, mixing until just combined. Be careful not to overmix; a few lumps are fine. Refrigerate the batter for 15-20 minutes to allow it to rest and become crispier.

instructions.title: Prepare Seafood and Vegetables
instructions.text: Clean and pat dry all the seafood. Cut shrimp and squid into bite-sized pieces if not already. Slice green onions into 3-4 inch lengths and thinly slice the onion. Mince garlic and chop green onion for the dipping sauce.

instructions.title: Make the Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: While the batter rests, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, toasted sesame seeds, minced garlic, and chopped green onion in a small bowl. Stir well and set aside.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the entire bottom of the pan. The pan should be hot enough that a drop of water sizzles instantly.

instructions.title: Assemble the Pancake
instructions.text: Pour about 1/2 to 3/4 cup of batter into the hot pan, spreading it slightly to form a thin, even layer. Quickly arrange a generous amount of green onion lengths evenly over the batter, followed by a mix of the prepared seafood and sliced onions. Lightly drizzle a little more batter over the toppings to help bind them.

instructions.title: Cook and Flip
instructions.text: Cook for 5-7 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy, and the edges begin to curl. Drizzle a little more oil around the edges. Carefully flip the pancake using a large spatula. Press down gently to ensure even cooking.

instructions.title: Finish Cooking and Serve
instructions.text: Cook the other side for another 5-7 minutes, or until golden brown and the seafood is fully cooked. For extra crispiness, you can flip it back and forth a couple more times, adding a little oil each time. Transfer the Haemul Pajeon to a cutting board, slice into wedges, and serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce. Repeat for remaining batter and ingredients.

tips: For the crispiest Pajeon, always use very cold water or broth for the batter and let it rest in the refrigerator for at least 15 minutes. This helps develop the gluten less and makes for a lighter, crispier texture.

Do not overcrowd your pan with too many ingredients. While it's tempting to load up on seafood and green onions, a balanced ratio ensures the pancake cooks evenly and remains crispy. Use a good quality non-stick pan and plenty of oil.

Serve Haemul Pajeon immediately after cooking to enjoy its maximum crispiness. Pair it with makgeolli (Korean rice wine) for an authentic Korean dining experience, or serve as an appetizer with other Korean dishes.
article:
Discover the culinary delight that is Haemul Pajeon, a beloved Korean seafood pancake that tantalizes taste buds with its crispy edges, tender interior, and generous medley of fresh seafood and vibrant green onions. Whether you've savored it at a bustling Korean street food stall, a cozy restaurant, or are simply curious to try your hand at this iconic dish, Haemul Pajeon offers a symphony of textures and flavors that are truly irresistible. Often enjoyed with a refreshing glass of makgeolli (Korean rice wine), it's more than just a pancake – it's an experience, a comfort food, and a testament to the rich culinary heritage of Korea. If you're searching for the ultimate Haemul Pajeon recipe to recreate this masterpiece at home, you’ve come to the right place. Get ready to embark on a delicious journey that will transform your kitchen into a Korean culinary haven.

## What is Haemul Pajeon?
At its heart, Haemul Pajeon is a savory Korean pancake, where "Haemul" translates to seafood and "Pajeon" specifically refers to a scallion pancake. Unlike the thinner, more delicate French crêpes or American pancakes, Pajeon is robust, hearty, and packed with flavor and texture. The defining characteristic of Haemul Pajeon is the abundant use of long, vibrant green onions, which are often laid out almost whole across the pan, then bound together by a light, savory batter.

Historically, pancakes like Pajeon have been a staple in Korean cuisine, adapting to regional ingredients and preferences. Haemul Pajeon, in particular, showcases Korea's strong connection to its coastal regions and the bounty of its seas. It’s not uncommon to find variations featuring different types of seafood depending on what’s fresh and available. What makes it stand out from other Korean pancakes like Kimchi Jeon (kimchi pancake) or Bindaetteok (mung bean pancake) is its emphasis on the sweet, briny notes of seafood combined with the sharp, fresh bite of green onions, all encased in a satisfyingly crispy crust. It's a dish that perfectly balances simplicity with profound flavor, making it a perennial favorite for gatherings, rainy days, or simply as a delicious snack or appetizer.

## Ingredients You'll Need for Haemul Pajeon
Crafting the perfect Haemul Pajeon begins with selecting fresh, quality ingredients. This Haemul Pajeon recipe calls for a balance of fresh seafood, crisp vegetables, and a well-seasoned batter.

For the Batter:
*   1 cup all-purpose flour
*   1/2 cup rice flour (or tempura flour for extra crispiness)
*   1 large egg
*   1 1/2 cups ice-cold water or anchovy broth (cold water helps with crispiness)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1/4 teaspoon black pepper

For the Seafood & Vegetables:
*   1 bunch (about 8-10 stalks) green onions, cut into 3-4 inch lengths
*   1/2 medium onion, thinly sliced
*   1 red chili, thinly sliced (optional, for a touch of heat and color)
*   1 cup mixed seafood, bite-sized pieces (e.g., shrimp, squid rings, mussels, oysters). Ensure shrimp are peeled and deveined, and squid is cleaned and scored.
*   Vegetable oil, for cooking

For the Dipping Sauce (Yangnyeom Ganjang):
*   2 tablespoons soy sauce
*   1 tablespoon rice vinegar
*   1 teaspoon sesame oil
*   1 teaspoon gochugaru (Korean chili flakes), optional
*   1/2 teaspoon toasted sesame seeds
*   1 clove garlic, minced (optional)

Having these ingredients prepped and ready will make the cooking process smooth and enjoyable, bringing you one step closer to a delightful homemade Haemul Pajeon.

## Step-by-Step Guide to Making Haemul Pajeon
Follow this Haemul Pajeon recipe meticulously for a truly authentic and delicious outcome.

1.  **Prepare the Seafood and Vegetables:** If using frozen seafood, thaw it thoroughly and pat dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture. This is crucial for crispiness. Cut green onions into 3-4 inch pieces. Thinly slice the onion and red chili (if using). Set aside.
2.  **Make the Batter:** In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, rice flour, salt, and pepper. Gradually add the ice-cold water or broth, whisking constantly until a smooth, thin batter forms. Be careful not to overmix; a few lumps are fine. Whisk in the egg until just combined. The cold liquid is key to achieving a crispy texture.
3.  **Heat the Pan:** Place a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 3-4 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the entire bottom of the pan generously. The oil needs to be hot enough to sizzle immediately when the batter is added.
4.  **Assemble and Cook the Pancake:** Once the oil is shimmering, carefully arrange a layer of green onion pieces across the pan. You can lay them parallel or in a criss-cross pattern, ensuring an even layer. Scatter a portion of the sliced onion and red chili over the green onions.
5.  **Add Seafood and Batter:** Evenly distribute a quarter to a third of your mixed seafood over the vegetables. Then, pour about 1/2 to 3/4 cup of the prepared batter over the seafood and vegetables, ensuring it covers everything and binds the ingredients together. Spread it gently to form an even, round pancake.
6.  **Cook Until Golden:** Let the pancake cook undisturbed for 4-5 minutes, or until the edges begin to turn golden brown and crispy, and the top looks mostly set. You can gently lift an edge with a spatula to check the color.
7.  **Flip and Finish:** Drizzle a little more oil around the edges of the pancake, then carefully flip it over. Press down gently with your spatula to ensure good contact with the pan and to encourage crispiness. Cook for another 4-5 minutes on the second side, until it’s golden brown, crispy, and the seafood is cooked through.
8.  **Repeat:** Slide the cooked Haemul Pajeon onto a cutting board. Repeat the process with the remaining ingredients, adding more oil to the pan as needed for each pancake.
9.  **Make the Dipping Sauce:** While the pancakes are cooking, combine all dipping sauce ingredients in a small bowl and mix well.

## Tips for a Perfectly Crispy Haemul Pajeon
Achieving that signature crispy exterior and tender interior for your Haemul Pajeon is key. These tips will elevate your Haemul Pajeon recipe from good to absolutely fantastic:

*   **Cold Batter is Crucial:** Always use ice-cold water or broth for your batter. The temperature difference between the cold batter and hot oil creates steam, resulting in a lighter, crispier texture. You can even chill your flour beforehand.
*   **The Right Flour Mix:** While all-purpose flour provides structure, incorporating rice flour or tempura flour adds an extra layer of crispiness. Rice flour makes the pancake lighter and less dense.
*   **Generous Oil:** Don't be shy with the oil! Haemul Pajeon benefits from being almost shallow-fried. This ensures a beautifully golden and crispy crust.
*   **Hot Pan:** Ensure your pan is very hot before adding the batter. A sizzling pan means instant crisping.
*   **Don't Overcrowd:** Cook one pancake at a time in a large skillet. Overcrowding lowers the pan temperature and leads to soggy pancakes.
*   **Press Down:** After flipping, gently press the pancake down with your spatula. This helps to flatten it slightly, ensuring even cooking and maximum crispiness.
*   **Serve Immediately:** Haemul Pajeon is best enjoyed fresh off the pan. The crispiness diminishes over time, so serve it hot!

## Serving Suggestions & Dipping Sauce
Once your Haemul Pajeon is perfectly golden and crispy, transfer it to a cutting board and slice it into bite-sized wedges or squares. It's traditionally served immediately, piping hot.

The accompanying dipping sauce (Yangnyeom Ganjang) is an essential part of the experience. The simple blend of soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil provides a tangy, savory counterpoint to the rich pancake. The optional addition of gochugaru and minced garlic adds a touch of heat and aromatic depth, elevating the overall flavor profile.

Haemul Pajeon makes a fantastic appetizer or a light meal on its own. It's a popular "anju" (food served with alcohol) in Korea, particularly cherished alongside makgeolli. The milky, slightly sweet and tangy rice wine provides a refreshing contrast to the savory pancake. You can also serve it with a side of kimchi or other simple banchan (side dishes) for a complete Korean dining experience.

## Haemul Pajeon Variations and Substitutions
While the classic Haemul Pajeon recipe is beloved, its versatility allows for numerous delicious variations and substitutions to suit different tastes and dietary needs.

*   **Seafood Choices:** Feel free to customize your seafood mix based on availability and preference. Clams, oysters, mussels, small scallops, or even imitation crab meat can be added. Just ensure all seafood is fresh and cut into small, manageable pieces.
*   **Vegetable Additions:** Beyond green onions and regular onion, you can incorporate other vegetables. Thinly sliced carrots, bell peppers (red or yellow for color), zucchini, or even mushrooms like shiitake or oyster mushrooms can add extra flavor and texture.
*   **Spicy Kick:** For those who love heat, finely chopped Korean green chilies (cheongyang gochu) can be mixed into the batter or scattered over the pancake before cooking. A dash of gochujang (Korean chili paste) in the batter can also add a subtle warmth.
*   **Vegetarian Pajeon:** To make a vegetarian version, simply omit the seafood and increase the amount of vegetables. Kimchi Pajeon (kimchi pancake) or Buchu Jeon (chive pancake) are popular and delicious vegetarian alternatives.
*   **Gluten-Free Option:** For a gluten-free Haemul Pajeon, use only rice flour or a gluten-free all-purpose flour blend for the batter. Ensure all other ingredients are also gluten-free.

No matter how you customize it, the essence of Haemul Pajeon – a delightful combination of crispy batter, fresh ingredients, and savory satisfaction – remains.

Haemul Pajeon is more than just a dish; it's a celebration of Korean flavors and textures. With its crispy exterior, tender interior, and a delightful mix of fresh seafood and aromatic green onions, it’s a culinary masterpiece that’s surprisingly accessible to make at home. Following this comprehensive guide, you’re now equipped with the ultimate Haemul Pajeon recipe and all the tips and tricks to create a perfectly crispy and flavorful pancake. So gather your ingredients, fire up your skillet, and get ready to savor the incredible taste of homemade Korean seafood pancake. Enjoy!
